falsifiableQuestion: Across rounds, does verifier-gated expert iteration KEEP at least one Lean-verified AND novel proof, with the anti-gaming check showing no kernel-gaming?

antiGaming.interpretation: structurally near-vacuous: the train and held-out verifiers are the same sound kernel oracle; a non-zero drop would indicate toolchain non-determinism, not reward hacking

rounds_detail.note: kept 3 verified+novel proof(s) into the expert corpus

interpretation: Expert iteration kept 3 verified+novel proof(s) across 2 round(s), gated by the scripted stub applier (NOT real Lean). The anti-gaming check confirmed no kernel-gaming (drop 0.0; structurally near-vacuous). candidateOnly: a --stub run exercises the LOOP, not a real verification. It does NOT close the bet.
successCriterion: >=1 Lean-verified proof with novelty=true on a HELD-OUT theorem, reproduced across seeds, under the no-overclaim gate. A --stub run is NOT sufficient: it verifies via a scripted applier, not the real kernel. See agi-proof/proof-search/LEAN-EXPERT-ITERATION-SETUP.md.
